output.final_deliverable:
I searched and attempted to visit candidate news items about “artificial intelligence regulation.” From the merged results I can extract the following items and what is missing for each (I include the URL citations shown in the merged results):

1) Candidate: "Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence – The White House"
- Headline (from search result): Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence – The White House
- Publication date: MISSING in merged results
- Main topic / key point: MISSING (search result title implies a national policy framework for AI, but article body or summary is not present in the merged results)
- Source website: whitehouse.gov
- URL: https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/12/eliminating-state-law-obstruction-of-nationa
- Notes: Only the search-result title and URL were returned in the merged data; the article text and date were not included.

2) Candidate: "AI legislation in the US: A 2026 overview - SIG"
- Headline (from search result): AI legislation in the US: A 2026 overview - SIG
- Publication date: MISSING in merged results
- Main topic / key point: MISSING (title indicates an overview of US AI legislation, but the merged results do not include the article contents or a date)
- Source website: softwareimprovementgroup.com
- URL: https://www.softwareimprovementgroup.com/blog/us-ai-legislation-overview/
- Notes: Only the search-result title and URL were returned in the merged data.

3) Candidate: The Guardian technology section (visited)
- Headline: MISSING (the visit returned the Guardian technology section page but no specific article headline was selected or captured)
- Publication date: MISSING
- Main topic / key point: MISSING
- Source website: theguardian.com (Technology section)
- URL visited: https://www.theguardian.com/technology
- Notes: The merged results show a successful visit to the Guardian technology section (links_count and sample links) but did not capture any specific article text, headline, or publication date.

Other visit attempt:
- Reuters technology section: attempted visit failed with HTTP 401 Unauthorized. URL: https://www.reuters.com/technology/ (error returned in merged results: "HTTP 401 Unauthorized (authentication required): HTTP fetch failed")

Summary / comparison of perspectives: MISSING (cannot produce a reliable comparison)
- Reason: The merged results provided only search-result titles/URLs for two items and a section page for The Guardian; they did not include article bodies, publication dates, or explicit summaries. Because the core article content and dates are absent from the merged results, I cannot reliably extract the articles' main points or compare perspectives across sources.

Suggested next steps (based only on items already discovered in the merged results):
- Revisit and fetch the full White House page at the URL above to capture headline, publication date, and article text.
- Visit the SIG blog URL to capture the article text and date for the 2026 overview.
- On The Guardian technology page, open one or more specific articles about AI regulation and capture headline, date, and main points.
- Re-attempt the Reuters technology URL (the visit failed with HTTP 401) or fetch a specific Reuters article URL about AI regulation.

If you want, I can re-run the visits now (open the three URLs above and extract headline, date, and main point from each) and then produce the requested comparison.
output.action_summary: I located three candidate sources in the merged results (a White House page, a Software Improvement Group blog post, and The Guardian technology section) and attempted to visit Reuters but the fetch failed. The merged results include only titles/URLs or a section page; they do not include publication dates or article bodies. Because the necessary article text and dates are missing from the merged results, I cannot extract the publication dates or main points nor compare perspectives yet. Next step: fetch the full articles at the listed URLs (White House, SIG blog, and specific Guardian articles) and re-run the extraction; I can do that now if you’d like.

details.intent: After the visit nodes return extracted data, synthesize the headlines, dates, main topics, and sources and produce a short comparative summary of the different perspectives.
target_facts: Summaries of each visited article (headline, date, key point, source)
Comparison of perspectives: differences in framing, policy emphasis, actors quoted, tone (e.g., supportive, skeptical, caution), and any conflicts in reported facts.
Short (2-4 sentence) overall summary highlighting common themes and key divergences.
details.extraction_plan: 1) Receive structured outputs from both visit nodes. 2) Compare main topics and quoted actors. 3) Note differences in framing or emphasis (e.g., regulatory urgency vs. industry concerns). 4) Produce a concise comparative summary plus a short recommendation for any follow-up visits (e.g., if a policymaker statement needs its original source).
details.verification_steps: 1) Ensure each claim in the summary references an extracted field from the visit outputs (headline, date, quote). 2) If ambiguity remains, flag which article/quote caused it and recommend re-visiting that URL.
details.rationale: A think node is required after data-gathering to synthesize and compare perspectives. This prevents premature conclusions and ensures the summary is grounded in the exact extracted facts from visited articles.

details.verification_steps: 1) Ensure each claim in the summary references an extracted field from the visit outputs (headline, date, quote). 2) If ambiguity remains, flag which article/quote caused it and recommend re-visiting that URL.
details.rationale: A think node is required after data-gathering to synthesize and compare perspectives. This prevents premature conclusions and ensures the summary is grounded in the exact extracted facts from visited articles.
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

details.intent: Store the final synthesized summary and the extracted article metadata (headline, date, key point, source, and article URL) into memory for future retrieval.
target_facts: Structured record for each visited article: {headline, date, key point, source, url}
details.extraction_plan: After the think node produces the final structured outputs, save them as a single memory item labeled 'recent AI regulation articles summary' with individual article records and the comparative summary.
details.verification_steps: 1) Confirm all fields are present for each article before saving. 2) Confirm the comparative summary references only saved article fields.
details.rationale: Saving the evidence and summary enables quick future retrieval and ensures we maintain provenance (which articles were used and what they said).
